response: The Old German Shepherd Dog has origins outside of the United States. It is a long-haired variation of the German Shepherd Dog, which traces its lineage to an experiment conducted in Czechoslovakia in 1955. In contrast, the Black Mouth Cur is a hunting and cattle dog with origins in the Southern United States. The Old German Shepherd Dog is not recognized as a separate breed by the Fédération Cynologique Internationale, while the Black Mouth Cur is a distinct breed with its own origins.
